## QUESTION 1

1. Identify the top 10 players with the longest Major League Baseball careers. Include their playerID, first_name, last_name, career_length

_Nick Altrock holds the longest career in this dataset at 35 years. This is a unique case in baseball history, as he had a long gap between his primary pitching years and several hitting appearances later on. Nolan Ryan appears in the top 10 as well, with a 27 year career, showing his status as one of the most durable players in the sport. Only 4 players exceed the 30 year mark, showing how rare that longevity is in professional sports._

::: {#1602b93f .cell execution_count=3}
``` {.python .cell-code}
people = pl.read_database("SELECT playerID, nameFirst, nameLast, debut, finalGame FROM people", con)

longest_careers = (
    people
    .with_columns([
        pl.col("debut").str.to_date("%Y-%m-%d", strict=False).dt.year().alias("start_year"),
        pl.col("finalGame").str.to_date("%Y-%m-%d", strict=False).dt.year().alias("end_year")
    ])
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("end_year") - pl.col("start_year")).alias("career_length")
    )
    .filter(pl.col("career_length").is_not_null())
    .sort("career_length", descending=True)
    .select([
        pl.col("playerID"),
        pl.col("nameFirst").alias("first_name"),
        pl.col("nameLast").alias("last_name"),
        pl.col("career_length")
    ])
    .head(10)
)

print(longest_careers)
```

## QUESTION 2

2. Write an SQL query that pulls the tables you need into a polars dataframe. Then, use python polars to create a summary table showing the average salary for each position (e.g., pitcher, catcher, outfielder). Position information can be found in the fielding table in the POS column. Include the following columns: position, average_salary, total_players, highest_salary. The highest_salary column should display the highest salary ever earned by a player in that position. Additionally, add a new column to the summary table you just created to label the average salary: if the average salary is above $3 million, categorize it as “High Salary”; if the average salary is between $2 million and $3 million, categorize it as “Medium Salary”; otherwise, categorize it as “Low Salary.” Order the table by average salary in descending order.

_The "High Salary" position is first base, which stands out as the highest paid position on average at roughly $3.3 million. While pitchers have one of the highest individual salaries ever earned ($33M), their average salary is only $1.9M due to the volume of players in that position. Catchers have one of the lowest average salaries at $1.4M due to their iffensive production being lower than infielders or outfielders, and it usually has a high physical toll on them leading to shorter careers. The "Medium Salary" bracket includes mainly outfielders, third basemen, and shortstops, all averaging between $2M-$3M._

::: {#b01ac43f .cell execution_count=4}
``` {.python .cell-code}
fielding = pl.read_database("SELECT playerID, yearID, POS, G FROM fielding", con)
salaries = pl.read_database("SELECT playerID, yearID, salary FROM salaries", con)


primary_positions = (
    fielding
    .sort("G", descending=True)
    .group_by(["playerID", "yearID"])
    .first() 
)


salary_summary = (
    salaries
    .join(primary_positions, on=["playerID", "yearID"], how="inner")
    .group_by("POS")
    .agg([
        pl.col("salary").mean().alias("average_salary"),
        pl.col("playerID").n_unique().alias("total_players"),
        pl.col("salary").max().alias("highest_salary")
    ])

    .with_columns(
        pl.when(pl.col("average_salary") > 3_000_000)
        .then(pl.lit("High Salary"))
        .when(pl.col("average_salary").is_between(2_000_000, 3_000_000))
        .then(pl.lit("Medium Salary"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("Low Salary"))
        .alias("salary_category")
    )
    .sort("average_salary", descending=True)
    .rename({"POS": "position"})
)

print(salary_summary)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

╞══════════╪════════════════╪═══════════════╪════════════════╪═════════════════╡

│ SS       ┆ 2.0075e6       ┆ 370           ┆ 2.26e7         ┆ Medium Salary   │
│ P        ┆ 1.9381e6       ┆ 2556          ┆ 3.3e7          ┆ Low Salary      │
│ 2B       ┆ 1.8016e6       ┆ 483           ┆ 2.4e7          ┆ Low Salary      │
│ C        ┆ 1.4305e6       ┆ 404           ┆ 2.3e7          ┆ Low Salary      │
